title: The One Where Chandler Can't Remember Which Sister

jokes.text: Joey's overly confident magic trick presentation followed by the obvious reveal
jokes.setup: Joey presents himself as a skilled magician with dramatic flair
jokes.punchline: The trick is just looking at the card quickly
jokes.explanation: Classic Joey obliviousness - he thinks he's amazing at magic when he's just doing the most obvious thing. Good character integration.

jokes.text: Real magic does exist.
jokes.setup: Joey successfully guesses the card
jokes.punchline: Joey genuinely believes he performed real magic
jokes.explanation: Joey's sincere belief in his own magical powers is perfectly in character and absurd.

jokes.text: I looked at it so fast, it was invisible to the naked eye. I just did it. Just did it again.
jokes.setup: Joey claims to explain his magic trick
jokes.punchline: His 'explanation' is that he looked so fast it was invisible, and he keeps 'doing it again'
jokes.explanation: Joey's complete lack of understanding of what constitutes an explanation, delivered with total confidence.

jokes.text: Anybody got a length of rope about six feet long...little noose at the end?
jokes.setup: Chandler asks for rope in a casual way
jokes.punchline: The 'little noose at the end' reveals he's joking about suicide
jokes.explanation: Classic Chandler dark humor with excellent economy - the casual delivery makes the dark punchline funnier.

jokes.text: I almost feel bad for whipping that kid's pretzel at them.
jokes.setup: Chandler saw Janice looking happy with her husband
jokes.punchline: His response was to throw a pretzel at them, taken from a random kid
jokes.explanation: Funny specificity - taking someone else's pretzel to throw adds absurd pettiness to Chandler's jealousy.

jokes.text: Any excuse to tell that story, you know?
jokes.setup: Ross tells story about seeing Katherine with another woman and having sex with both
jokes.punchline: Ross admits he just looks for excuses to brag about the threesome
jokes.explanation: Ross's transparent humble-bragging is in character, though the setup is a bit forced.

jokes.text: Then if anybody should have a party, it should be him.
jokes.setup: Monica says it's Joey's birthday party
jokes.punchline: Chandler's obvious statement delivered as if it's profound wisdom
jokes.explanation: Mild Chandler sarcasm but not particularly strong or memorable.

jokes.text: I'm not suppose to drink coffee. It makes me gassy. I know.
jokes.setup: Boss mentions he shouldn't drink coffee
jokes.punchline: TMI about gas problems, with Rachel's uncomfortable acknowledgment
jokes.explanation: Basic workplace awkwardness humor - functional but not inspired.

jokes.text: I need these hangers separated ASAP.
jokes.setup: Boss promises Rachel a fashion-related project after building up her hopes
jokes.punchline: The 'project' is mindless hanger sorting
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff structure - the deflation from 'fashion project' to hangers is well-executed.

jokes.text: Order up! Yentl soup, James Beans and a Howdy, hold the Doody.
jokes.setup: Gunther calls out food orders
jokes.punchline: The menu items are puns on celebrity names and catchphrases
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that fits the coffee shop setting, though puns can be hit-or-miss.

jokes.text: Actually, that's a Barbra Streisand-wich. What? I named that one.
jokes.setup: Rachel dismissively calls it 'your little hamburger'
jokes.punchline: Mark proudly reveals he named the Streisand sandwich
jokes.explanation: Follows up the menu puns nicely and shows Mark's pride in his wordplay.

jokes.text: Do you want my pickle?
jokes.setup: Rachel learns Mark works at Bloomingdale's and might help her
jokes.punchline: She desperately offers him her pickle in gratitude
jokes.explanation: Rachel's desperation is funny and in character, though the joke is simple.

jokes.text: Stick out your tongue! Take off your shirt!
jokes.setup: Chandler appears very drunk at the party
jokes.punchline: The sisters immediately start making demands of drunk Chandler
jokes.explanation: Basic drunk person comedy - sets up the situation but isn't particularly clever.

jokes.text: Yeah, Jell-O. Just like Mom used to make.
jokes.setup: Someone mentions the Jell-O shots are pure vodka
jokes.punchline: Chandler implies his mother made alcoholic Jell-O
jokes.explanation: Good Chandler moment - references his dysfunctional family in a darkly funny way.

jokes.text: Sounds like Mark Something wants to have some sex!
jokes.setup: Rachel describes Mark helping her for 'no reason'
jokes.punchline: Ross's blunt, jealous assessment of Mark's motives
jokes.explanation: Ross's jealousy expressed in his awkward, direct way. The phrasing 'have some sex' is funny.

jokes.text: No, only for sex. Thank you.
jokes.setup: Ross asks Joey if men are ever nice to strange women for no reason
jokes.punchline: Joey's immediate, definitive 'No, only for sex' with Ross's satisfied 'thank you'
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy and structure - Joey's blunt honesty provides exactly what Ross wanted to hear.

jokes.text: I was wearing my 'I Heart Ross' sandwich board and ringing my bell.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if she told Mark about him
jokes.punchline: Rachel sarcastically describes advertising their relationship in the most obvious way possible
jokes.explanation: Good visual imagery and sarcastic response to Ross's jealous questioning.

jokes.text: How many of that girl are you seeing?
jokes.setup: Chandler is very drunk
jokes.punchline: His confused question about Joey's multiple sisters
jokes.explanation: Classic drunk confusion - simple but effective for showing how intoxicated Chandler is.

jokes.text: That's the same drink I made myself right after I shot my husband. Okay, I don't know how to talk to you.
jokes.setup: Chandler mentions vodka and cranberry juice
jokes.punchline: Sister casually mentions shooting her husband, Chandler's immediate retreat
jokes.explanation: Excellent dark humor with perfect setup - the casual murder confession followed by Chandler's honest reaction.

jokes.text: Why must we dial so speedily, anyway? Why rush through life? Why can't we savor the precious moments?
jokes.setup: Chandler is deleting Janice from speed dial
jokes.punchline: Drunk Chandler gets philosophical about speed dialing
jokes.explanation: Drunk Chandler finding deep meaning in mundane technology is funny and in character.

jokes.text: Those are some huge breasts you have!
jokes.setup: Chandler is having a deep philosophical moment
jokes.punchline: Immediately switches to inappropriate comment about breasts
jokes.explanation: Drunk inappropriate comment - predictable but the sudden shift from philosophy to crudeness works.

jokes.text: I mean, when he left, I actually used the phrase: 'Hummina, hummina, hummina.'
jokes.setup: Monica describes how attractive Mark is
jokes.punchline: She admits to actually saying cartoon-style attraction sounds
jokes.explanation: Monica's honesty about using cartoon attraction sounds is endearing and funny.

jokes.text: Well, I assume I'll have to take showers with him. But, you know, that's true of any job.
jokes.setup: Ross asks if she really thinks Mark wants nothing
jokes.punchline: Rachel casually mentions shower expectations as if it's normal workplace behavior
jokes.explanation: Rachel's matter-of-fact delivery of something absurd works well as a response to Ross's paranoia.

jokes.text: Well, my apartment isn't there anymore because I drank it.
jokes.setup: Asked how he's feeling after drinking
jokes.punchline: Chandler's poetic description of being so drunk his apartment disappeared
jokes.explanation: Excellent drunk logic with poetic absurdity - 'I drank my apartment' is memorably bizarre.

jokes.text: I can't remember which sister.
jokes.setup: Chandler admits to fooling around with Joey's sister
jokes.punchline: The revelation that he doesn't know which one makes it worse
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - just when you think the situation can't get worse, this revelation makes it catastrophically funny.

jokes.text: You see? You can't tell which is which either!
jokes.setup: Monica and Phoebe try to help identify which sister
jokes.punchline: Chandler points out they're also confused about which is which
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that extends the premise - Chandler finding validation in others' confusion.

jokes.text: That was me.
jokes.setup: Chandler thinks he remembers sticking his tongue down someone's throat
jokes.punchline: Monica reveals it was her he kissed
jokes.explanation: Excellent economy - two words that completely change the situation and add another layer of embarrassment.

jokes.text: When I drink, sometimes I get overly friendly and I'm sorry.
jokes.setup: Monica just revealed she kissed Chandler
jokes.punchline: She calmly explains it as getting 'overly friendly' when drinking
jokes.explanation: Monica's matter-of-fact explanation of her drunk behavior is endearingly awkward.

jokes.text: Yep, I'm in a tree.
jokes.setup: Joey enthusiastically says 'You and my sister, sitting in a tree!'
jokes.punchline: Chandler's flat acknowledgment of the metaphor
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chandler deadpan - taking Joey's childish enthusiasm completely literally and flatly.

jokes.text: What's it about? I don't remember.
jokes.setup: Phoebe says she finished her book
jokes.punchline: She immediately admits she doesn't remember what it was about
jokes.explanation: Classic Phoebe moment - her spacey personality perfectly captured in not remembering what she just read.

jokes.text: Where in Cuba?
jokes.setup: Ross suggests Chandler could hide in Cuba after telling Joey he moved to France
jokes.punchline: Chandler immediately starts planning the specifics of his Cuba escape
jokes.explanation: Shows Chandler's panic through his immediate focus on escape logistics rather than facing the problem.

jokes.text: She was, like, the sixth person to spit on Mussolini's hanging body.
jokes.setup: Joey warns Chandler not to upset his grandmother
jokes.punchline: The oddly specific and dark historical reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurd specificity - the exact ranking ('sixth person') makes this memorably bizarre and quotable.

jokes.text: They're in Vermont, buying cigarettes.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks where Joey's parents are
jokes.punchline: Joey's matter-of-fact explanation of their odd errand
jokes.explanation: Joey's casual mention of parents traveling to another state for cigarettes suggests family dysfunction.

jokes.text: He had to help his niece with her report on the pioneers. That is so made up!
jokes.setup: Rachel explains why Mark didn't call earlier
jokes.punchline: Ross immediately declares the innocent excuse is fake
jokes.explanation: Ross's paranoid jealousy leading him to declare an innocent excuse as obviously fabricated.

jokes.text: High collar and baggy pants say, 'I'm a pro!'
jokes.setup: Rachel needs to figure out what to wear for the interview
jokes.punchline: Monica's advice to dress as unsexy as possible
jokes.explanation: Monica trying to help Rachel avoid Mark's advances through strategic dowdy dressing.

jokes.text: Now he's gonna prep her...as in what you do before you surgically remove the boyfriend!
jokes.setup: Mark is going to 'prep' Rachel for the interview
jokes.punchline: Ross's paranoid interpretation of 'prep' as surgical boyfriend removal
jokes.explanation: Ross's jealous paranoia taken to medical extremes - good character work showing his spiral.

jokes.text: You grow up.
jokes.setup: Monica tells Ross to 'grow up' after his paranoid rant
jokes.punchline: Ross's childish comeback
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective - Ross proving Monica's point about needing to grow up with his response.

jokes.text: That dies with me. So will I.
jokes.setup: Chandler asks for the tiramisu recipe
jokes.punchline: Grandma says the recipe dies with her, Chandler says so will he
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor callback to the Mussolini reference - Chandler expecting death from this family.

jokes.text: God, Mary Angela was right. You do have the softest lips. You're not Mary Angela?
jokes.setup: Wrong sister kisses Chandler and compliments his lips
jokes.punchline: Chandler's horror at realizing he's with the wrong sister again
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation - just when Chandler thinks he's figured it out, it gets worse. The lip compliment adds absurdity.

jokes.text: You're it! Now run and hide!
jokes.setup: Joey appears and Chandler needs to escape
jokes.punchline: Chandler desperately tries to turn the situation into a children's game
jokes.explanation: Chandler's desperate attempt to avoid confrontation through childish games is funny and in character.

jokes.text: I was really drunk and you guys all look really similar.
jokes.setup: Chandler tries to explain his confusion about the sisters
jokes.punchline: His 'explanation' just makes it worse by saying they all look the same
jokes.explanation: Chandler's attempt to explain just digs the hole deeper - realistic awkward damage control.

jokes.text: I say punch him, Joey! Yeah, punch him!
jokes.setup: The sisters hear about Chandler's behavior
jokes.punchline: They immediately call for violence
jokes.explanation: Standard angry mob reaction - functional but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Cookie, now you can punch him! What?
jokes.setup: Joey has forgiven Chandler and they've worked things out
jokes.punchline: Sister gives permission to punch him anyway, Chandler's confusion
jokes.explanation: Sister still wants violence despite resolution - shows family dynamics don't follow friendship rules.

jokes.text: You know, this building's on my paper route, so...
jokes.setup: Ross unexpectedly appears at Rachel's interview building
jokes.punchline: His obviously made-up excuse about a paper route
jokes.explanation: Ross's stalking behavior disguised with a transparent lie fits his jealous character arc.

jokes.text: Eavesdropping? Pheebs, the ceiling tiles were falling down.
jokes.setup: Phoebe asks if Monica was eavesdropping on her date
jokes.punchline: Monica's defense that the noise was destroying the apartment
jokes.explanation: Good callback to the opening setup about the noisy neighbor, with Monica's practical concern.

jokes.text: Maybe the pogo stick likes it too.
jokes.setup: Monica suggests the guy is just 'jumping on a pogo stick'
jokes.punchline: Phoebe's innocent suggestion that the pogo stick enjoys it
jokes.explanation: Perfect Phoebe moment - her innocent interpretation of the obvious euphemism is endearingly naive.

jokes.text: If it's any consolation...he really did sound like he was having more fun with you.
jokes.setup: Monica tries to console Phoebe about the guys going upstairs
jokes.punchline: Her 'consolation' is awkwardly comparing the sounds of intimacy
jokes.explanation: Monica's attempt at comfort through awkward intimate comparisons is believably uncomfortable.

tmdb_overview: Ross is suspicious about Rachel's possible job offer from a good-looking stranger. A drunken Chandler fools around with one of Joey's sisters, but he can't remember which one.
